title: Add Activity Log Panel to Trading Bot UI

requirements.text: Add a backend query endpoint that returns a chronological activity log of bot events, including trade executions, order placements, order cancellations, errors, and bot start/stop events. Each log entry should include a timestamp, event type (e.g., 'trade', 'order_placed', 'order_cancelled', 'error', 'bot_started', 'bot_stopped'), and a descriptive message. Store the most recent 100 log entries in stable state.

quotes: Podemos adicionar um pequeno log. De atividades. Na interface. Que ajude a encontrar erros no processo?

acceptanceCriteria: A query function `getActivityLog` returns an array of log entries with timestamp, event type, and message fields.
Bot start and stop events are recorded in the log.
Order placement and cancellation events are recorded with order details.
Errors encountered during the trading loop are recorded with descriptive messages.
Log is capped at the 100 most recent entries to prevent unbounded growth.
Log entries persist across canister upgrades via stable storage.

requirements.text: Add an ActivityLogPanel frontend component that displays the bot activity log fetched from the backend, showing each entry with its timestamp (relative time), event type badge (color-coded: green for trades, yellow for orders, red for errors, neutral for status changes), and message. The panel should auto-refresh every 10 seconds using the existing React Query polling pattern from useQueries.ts and display a 'No activity yet' empty state when the log is empty.

quotes: Podemos adicionar um pequeno log. De atividades. Na interface. Que ajude a encontrar erros no processo?

acceptanceCriteria: ActivityLogPanel renders a scrollable list of log entries sorted by most recent first.
Each entry displays a relative timestamp, a color-coded event type badge, and a message.
Error events are visually distinct (red badge) to aid debugging.
Panel shows a 'No activity yet' empty state when there are no log entries.
Panel polls the backend every 10 seconds via React Query.
Loading skeleton is shown while data is being fetched on first load.

requirements.text: Integrate the ActivityLogPanel into the main App.tsx dashboard layout, placing it below the TradeHistoryPanel or in a prominent position in the grid so it is immediately visible without scrolling on desktop viewports.
acceptanceCriteria: ActivityLogPanel is rendered in App.tsx as part of the dashboard grid.
Panel is visible in the main viewport area on desktop without requiring scroll.
Layout remains responsive on smaller screens.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Follow the existing React Query polling pattern in useQueries.ts for the new activity log query hook.
Activity log storage in the backend must use stable variables to survive canister upgrades.
nonGoals: Real-time WebSocket or push-based log streaming.
Exporting or downloading the activity log.
Changing the existing trading logic or configuration.
